title: Chicago Crime, 1986–2026 — Forty Years Mapped Across All 77 Community Areas
description:
Forty years of reported crime in Chicago — from the 1991 peak to a city where reported crime has halved, mapped across all 77 community areas.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program (1986–2002) and the Chicago Police Department's CLEAR incident data (2003–2026). Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location, anonymized to the block by the City of Chicago. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — crime halved
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc, 1986–2025
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — 77 community areas, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest community areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ DATA SOURCES
• Chicago Police Department (CLEAR) incidents, data.cityofchicago.org (2001–present)
• FBI Uniform Crime Reporting / Crime Data Explorer (1986–2002), ORI ILCPD0000
• Community area boundaries: City of Chicago

This video uses data that has been modified for use from its original source, www.cityofchicago.org, the official website of the City of Chicago. The City of Chicago makes no claims as to the content, accuracy, timeliness, or completeness of any of the data provided. The data provided is subject to change at any time. It is understood that the data provided is being used at one's own risk.

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/chicago-il

Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI).

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share it with someone from Chicago.

#Chicago #CrimeData #DataVisualization #OpenData
